name: Garrovillas de Alconétar from Casar de Cáceres – circular by Vía de la Plata
name_original: Garrovillas de Alconétar desde Casar de Cáceres – circular por Vía de la Plata
region_intro: Touring cycling around Casar De Cáceres offers routes through the flat plains and gentle undulations of Extremadura, characterized by historic pathways and rural landscapes. The region features a mix of open countryside, agricultural areas, and proximity to cultural sites. Elevations are generally moderate, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Casar De Cáceres The most popular touring cycling route is Garrovillas de Alconétar from Casar de Cáceres – circular by Vía de la Plata, a 37.8 miles (61.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows a section of the historic Vía de la Plata. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Malpartida de Cáceres Old Town – Vostell Museum loop from Mercadona, a moderate 19.8 miles (31.9 km) path. This route explores cultural landmarks and the old town of Malpartida de Cáceres. Local touring cyclists also love the Malpartida de Cáceres – Los Barruecos Lagoons loop from Malpartida de Cáceres, a 16.3 miles (26.3 km) trail leading through Los Barruecos Lagoons, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Casar De Cáceres is defined by historic paths, rural landscapes, and cultural sites, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including more than 10 easy touring bike trails Casar De Cáceres. The routes in Casar De Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Casar De Cáceres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Casar De Cáceres?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes around Casar De Cáceres, offering a wide range of options for exploring the region's historic pathways and rural landscapes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Casar De Cáceres?
A: Yes, Casar De Cáceres offers more than 10 easy touring cycling routes. These trails are generally characterized by flat plains and gentle undulations, making them suitable for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Casar De Cáceres?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Casar De Cáceres is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the open countryside and historic sites.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Casar De Cáceres?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104258201/garrovillas-de-alconetar-from-casar-de-caceres-circular-by-via-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garrovillas de Alconétar from Casar de Cáceres – circular by Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a section of the historic Vía de la Plata.

Q: What cultural landmarks can I explore on a touring bike near Casar De Cáceres?
A: The region is rich in cultural landmarks. You can cycle to places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3783241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Co-Cathedral of Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/780383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Centre of Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38283548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpartida de Cáceres Old Town – Vostell Museum loop from Mercadona&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores the old town of Malpartida de Cáceres and its cultural sites.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through natural monuments or scenic areas?
A: Absolutely. The area features several natural monuments. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpartida de Cáceres – Los Barruecos Lagoons loop from Malpartida de Cáceres&lt;/a&gt; leads through the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6093807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barruecos Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique rock formations and lagoons, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lower Barrueco Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Casar De Cáceres?
A: The touring cycling routes around Casar De Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's blend of historic pathways, rural landscapes, and the opportunity to explore cultural sites like the Vía de la Plata, as well as the moderate elevations suitable for various abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, there are routes that extend further into the Extremaduran countryside. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Bujaco – Vía de la Plata loop from Casar de Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 145 km and offers an immersive experience along the historic Vía de la Plata.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate the historic Vía de la Plata?
A: Yes, the Vía de la Plata is a significant historic pathway in the region, and several touring cycling routes incorporate sections of it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104258201/garrovillas-de-alconetar-from-casar-de-caceres-circular-by-via-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garrovillas de Alconétar from Casar de Cáceres – circular by Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example that allows you to experience this ancient route.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that connect to the city of Cáceres?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead towards or through the historic city of Cáceres. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Cáceres – Historic Centre of Cáceres loop from Malpartida de Cáceres&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that takes you to the heart of Cáceres, allowing you to visit landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Bujaco&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4011664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres Museum and Cistern&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Casar De Cáceres?
A: The terrain around Casar De Cáceres is generally characterized by flat plains and gentle undulations, typical of Extremadura. You'll encounter a mix of open countryside, agricultural areas, and historic pathways. While elevations are mostly moderate, some routes may include more challenging climbs, offering variety for different skill levels.
tips: Rivera de Araya bridge on the EX-302 highway on the outskirts of Garrovillas de Alconétar. During draft, the river below is completely dry.
It is surprising to find such spectacular ruins. It is a convent dating from 1496. We hope that they can be cared for as they deserve and that the historical heritage that it represents is protected.

Garrovillas de Alconétar is a small town in the Tajo-Salor region. A strategic place on the left bank of the Tagus River, near its confluence with the Almonte River. Stop at the Plaza Mayor for food and drinks and admire the heritage buildings that surround the Plaza.

In this section you can see the milestones that marked the route of the old Roman road. Today cattle graze freely around them.
Like a highway, this stretch of wide, compact gravel road can be fast. Incredible scenery to cycle through without worrying about cars.
This is an area where you will not find any population for many kilometers.
